codeReview.summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff. Implementation behavior in inbox.py/inbox_client.py satisfies the feature requirements: client cleanup on exit/unmount, env-configurable poll interval, and resilient error handling for unreachable server/API failures while preserving TUI functionality and polling/send flows. Commit d7d9c88 adds targeted test coverage for these guarantees.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture/state docs are now out of date on polling configuration: they still describe a fixed 10s poll interval and config-file-based poll settings, while runtime behavior uses INBOX_POLL_INTERVAL.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:66 and :128-130 describe fixed 10s polling; .factory/library/architecture.md:170 and mission AGENTS.md:48 describe persisted config settings. Current code reads env var in inbox.py:39-45 and applies it via InboxApp.POLL_INTERVAL at inbox.py:317.

summary: Pass. No actionable code defects found in this feature review; added tests and current implementation align with architecture-hardening resilience goals. One shared-state documentation gap was recorded for polling configuration semantics.
